generated_oml:
instance AmbientTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Thermocouple sensor (DS18S20) positioned outside the incubator box to measure ambient room temperature."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AmbientTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HotSpotTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Thermocouple sensor (DS18S20) placed inside the incubator at the hottest point near the heatbed."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HotSpotTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance ColdSpotTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Thermocouple sensor (DS18S20) placed inside the incubator at the coldest point."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ColdSpotTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance AmbientTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AmbientTemperatureSensor
]

instance HotSpotTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HotSpotTemperatureSensor
]

instance ColdSpotTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ColdSpotTemperatureSensor
]

instance AmbientTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw ambient temperature reading transmitted via RabbitMQ as a lightweight JSON payload."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AmbientTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterEnabler, CalibrationEngineEnabler, CoSimulationEngineEnabler, DashboardEnabler, TimeSeriesDatabaseEnabler
]

instance HotSpotTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw hot spot temperature reading transmitted via RabbitMQ as a lightweight JSON payload."
    DTDFVocab:fromData HotSpotTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterEnabler, CalibrationEngineEnabler, CoSimulationEngineEnabler, DashboardEnabler, TimeSeriesDatabaseEnabler
]

instance ColdSpotTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw cold spot temperature reading transmitted via RabbitMQ as a lightweight JSON payload."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ColdSpotTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterEnabler, CalibrationEngineEnabler, CoSimulationEngineEnabler, DashboardEnabler, TimeSeriesDatabaseEnabler
]

instance KalmanFilterEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that integrates the Kalman filter module for heatbed state estimation and anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:enables KalmanFilterService
]

instance CalibrationEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that runs the automatic calibration routine to optimise ModelB parameters using temperature data."
    DTDFVocab:enables CalibrationService
]

instance CoSimulationEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that performs co‑simulation between the physical incubator and the digital twin for what‑if analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables CoSimulationService
]

instance DashboardEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that feeds time‑series data into the dashboard for real‑time visualization."
    DTDFVocab:enables DashboardService
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that writes incoming temperature data to a time‑series database for historical analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables TimeSeriesDatabaseService
]

instance SelfAdaptationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that orchestrates the self‑adaptation loop: experiment scheduling, model recalibration, Kalman filter update, and controller re‑configuration."
    DTDFVocab:enables SelfAdaptationService
]

instance KalmanFilterService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that fuses measured temperatures with ModelB to estimate the heatbed state and detect anomalies."
    DTDFVocab:provides HeatBedStateEstimationInsight, AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance CalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that performs on‑demand calibration of ModelB using temperature data from the three sensors."
    DTDFVocab:provides RecalibrateModelBAction
]

instance CoSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that runs co‑simulation of the physical twin and digital twin to evaluate controller parameter changes."
    DTDFVocab:provides RunWhatIfSimulationAction, WhatIfSimulationInsight
]

instance DashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that renders real‑time monitoring and visualization of incubator status on a dashboard."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualizationInsight
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabaseService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that stores incoming temperature data in a time‑series database for later analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeMonitoringInsight
]

instance SelfAdaptationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that closes the self‑adaptation loop: detects new objects, schedules experiments, recalibrates ModelB, updates Kalman filter, and re‑configures the controller."
    DTDFVocab:provides SelfAdaptationInsight, ReconfigureControllerAction, TriggerCalibrationAction, UpdateKalmanFilterAction, ScheduleExperimentAction
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that aggregates time‑series data for real‑time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeMonitoringInsight
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that uses the Kalman filter to detect deviations from predicted heatbed state."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance HeatBedStateEstimationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that outputs the estimated heatbed temperature and covariance from the Kalman filter."
    DTDFVocab:provides HeatBedStateEstimationInsight
]

instance WhatIfSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that performs what‑if simulations to optimise controller parameters."
    DTDFVocab:provides WhatIfSimulationInsight
]

instance DecisionMakingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that orchestrates algorithmic decision making and human‑in‑the‑loop actions."
    DTDFVocab:provides DecisionMakingInsight
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time monitoring of temperature data aggregated from the time‑series database."
]

instance RealTimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time visualization of incubator status via a dashboard consuming time‑series data from RabbitMQ."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Anomaly detection using a Kalman‑filter based heatbed state estimator deviations trigger alerts."
]

instance HeatBedStateEstimationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Heatbed state estimation: Kalman filter outputs estimated temperature and covariance, providing a confidence measure."
]

instance WhatIfSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "What‑if simulations: co‑simulation of the PT and DT to optimise controller parameters before deployment."
]

instance SelfAdaptationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Self‑adaptation loop that detects changes, schedules experiments, recalibrates ModelB, updates Kalman filter, and re‑configures the controller."
]

instance DecisionMakingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Decision making is both algorithmic (controller re‑configuration) and human‑in‑the‑loop (alerts can be acted upon by operators)."
]

instance ReconfigureControllerAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to re‑configure the bang‑bang controller parameters (LL, UL, H, C)."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance TriggerCalibrationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to trigger on‑demand calibration of ModelB."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RunWhatIfSimulationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to run a what‑if simulation to evaluate controller parameter changes."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance UpdateKalmanFilterAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to re‑tune the Kalman filter after detecting a new object."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RecalibrateModelBAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to recalibrate ModelB parameters using new data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ScheduleExperimentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to schedule an experiment when a new object is detected."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical simulation model with two free parameters: C_air and G_box."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationEngineEnabler
]

instance ModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical simulation model with four free parameters: C_air, G_box, C_heater, G_heater."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationEngineEnabler
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The controller component listens for control commands on RabbitMQ and forwards them to the low‑level driver, which translates them into actuator signals. Commands include turning the heatbed and fan on or off. The low‑level driver uses MOSFETs and optocouplers to isolate the Raspberry Pi from the high‑current actuators. The driver also publishes the current PT state back to RabbitMQ. No explicit validation, authentication, or conflict‑resolution mechanisms are described in the documents."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The physical incubator’s time scale is dominated by the fan’s air‑mixing time of approximately 2 s (calculated from a 0.02 m³/s airflow in a 0.03 m³ box). The low‑level driver polls the DS18S20 temperature sensors periodically, but the exact sampling interval is not specified in the documents. The controller implements a bang‑bang strategy with heating duration (H) and waiting duration (C) parameters; however, no numeric values or explicit timing constraints are provided. Communication between the physical twin (controller, driver) and the digital twin services occurs over RabbitMQ, which is described as lightweight and low‑latency, but no explicit latency requirements are documented. Overall, the DT operates on a sub‑second to second granularity, with the fan mixing time as the key physical time constant."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is orchestrated around a RabbitMQ message broker that connects the physical twin (controller and low‑level driver) with the digital twin services. The controller publishes actuator commands and consumes sensor data; the low‑level driver publishes sensor readings and consumes actuator commands. The Digital Twin services subscribe to these queues: a time‑series database ingests the data for real‑time visualization; a Kalman filter module estimates the unmeasured heat‑bed temperature using Model B; a calibration engine runs on demand to update Model B parameters; a co‑simulation engine performs what‑if simulations; and a self‑adaptation loop monitors anomalies, schedules experiments, re‑calibrates, and reconfigures the controller. All services are coordinated via RabbitMQ exchanges and queues, with the Digital Twin acting as a centralized orchestrator that processes messages, updates models, and publishes control decisions back to the physical twin."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT lifecycle for the incubator case study follows a multi‑stage process: 1) Physical Twin (PT) design and construction – an insulated styrofoam box with a heatbed, fan, Raspberry Pi controller, DS18S20 temperature sensors, MOSFET‑based PCB and optocouplers. 2) Model development – two thermal models (Model A with two free parameters and Model B with four free parameters) are derived from first‑principles and simplified assumptions. 3) Calibration – Model B is calibrated against measured temperature data using a calibration routine that updates the four free parameters to capture the heatbed’s thermal inertia. 4) Digital Twin (DT) integration – the calibrated model, a Kalman‑filter based heat‑bed state estimator, a real‑time data pipeline (RabbitMQ → time‑series database) and a dashboard are assembled. 5) Runtime monitoring – the DT continuously receives sensor data, runs the Kalman filter, flags anomalies, and visualises status. 6) Self‑adaptation loop – upon anomaly detection the DT schedules an experiment, re‑configures the controller, re‑collects data, re‑calibrates Model B, re‑tunes the Kalman filter, runs what‑if simulations to optimise controller parameters, and finally re‑deploys the updated controller. 7) Governance – the entire stack is open‑source, hosted on GitHub, and version‑controlled; changes to models, calibration scripts, and controller code are tracked via Git commits, enabling traceability and reproducibility."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model parameters were calibrated in a dedicated experiment using the optimized sensor layout. The calibration yielded specific values for C_air, G_box, C_heater, and G_heater. No additional validation or uncertainty quantification procedures are described in the documents."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "All components (controller, low‑level driver, RabbitMQ broker) run on a single Raspberry Pi 4 Model B. Communication between them uses the AMQP protocol via the locally hosted RabbitMQ server. The system operates on a single‑node topology with no clustering or redundancy. Bandwidth, latency, reliability, and security requirements are not specified in the documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital twin is deployed on‑premise on a Raspberry Pi 4 Model B. The software stack consists of the controller, low‑level driver, and RabbitMQ broker, all running directly on the Pi’s operating system. Computational resources are those of the Pi (quad‑core 1.5 GHz, 4 GB RAM). Storage is provided by the Pi’s SD card. No containerization, cloud deployment, or scalability mechanisms are described."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin integrates with external systems primarily through the RabbitMQ message broker, which is an open‑source, lightweight, on‑premises or cloud‑deployable message broker. The system also relies on a time‑series database (not specified which one) for storing sensor data and a dashboard for visualization. No other enterprise systems or external Digital Twins are referenced; the integration pattern is a publish/subscribe model over RabbitMQ, with the Digital Twin components acting as subscribers to the physical twin’s data streams and publishers of control decisions."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "No explicit industry standards, certification requirements, or interoperability frameworks are mentioned in the documents. The system relies on open‑source components (RabbitMQ, Raspberry Pi, DS18S20 sensors) and follows best practices for open‑source development, but no formal standard compliance is reported."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Safe protocol incorporated into the low‑level driver to keep the device in a safe state, ensuring that temperature data is read and commands are processed only when the device is in a safe condition."
]
